reviews.review_msg: One of the best professors in the Mathematics Department. He is humble, friendly, respectful, and very supportive of his students. I thoroughly enjoyed his course and would gladly take another course with him in the future.

Paul is an excellent professor, and he is extremely humble and honest. I really enjoyed his class, he really focuses on doing what's best for his students and structures everything around your success. I did not do very well but it was my own doing. I like that he writes the notes on the board, I don't like slides personally. Easy to get an A.
such a sweetie and his midterms were really easy. dont even have to attend lectures half the time. final was kinda bad but other than that he was fair.
I failed the diagnostic and still got a A- lmao very good teacher.
Fair grading and had good experience taking this course with Paul.
Strongly recommended
He is awesome, supportive, kind, and fair in grading.

He was a good Prof in general. His lectures were sometimes messy, but his exams aren't difficult at all - very fair and match well with your provided sample exams. He's also very helpful, supportive, and a nice guy overall

This man is the true goat and the greatest PUPPER of all time. Not only were his lectures amazing, but they were also amazing. He gave everyone an A+ for no reason which was pretty sick. He would also always talk about sports with us and do Messi's celebrations when people would ask questions! #TupperedUp #Math #nicee #cute #PaulTupperIsATallPupper
Great Professor. I highly recommend him if you get the opportunity to take a course with him.

Paul did a great job teaching this difficult class. The exam grades were low so it had to get scaled, but that benefited most people. His Instructor Questions were incredibly difficult though, almost unnecessarily compared to what you get tested on.

Was really sensitive about the class volume and chatter but content in class was often good enough to not need to read the textbook or review to do assignments and quizzes. Same exact format as calc 1 and calc 2. sometimes Paul's notes can be a bit messy but he answers questions honestly.

Paul is an excellent professor, with a caring and friendly personality. His lectures are dense and long, but he tries his best to convey the message in the best way possible and is always happy to stop and asnwer questions. He really has the students' best interest in mind at all times and it shows.

Paul is great at simplifying complex topics, but he often ran out of time and didn't cover all content in lectures. However, he would fill in the notes completely and post them on Canvas afterwards. Homework assignments were excessively long given they were used as question banks for quizzes. Exam difficulty varied greatly, but the final was fair.
Really nice guy, good at explaining concepts. First midterm was fair, second midterm was hard, final was alright. Just lots of practice and you'll be fine!

Prof. Tupper makes a difficult math class very straightforward. He gives lectures that are easy enough to follow with skeleton notes. You must spend a good amount of time doing practice problems from the textbook. My only complaint was having no formula sheet on the exams, but many math classes do this for some reason.
custom message 1
paul tupper made the midterm difficult and he instantly regret it the next day!

I get the sense that Paul would be a great prof for upper-division courses. In this course, however, I found it hard to grasp the intuition behind many concepts (and I loved calc 1 and 2). Also, there's a lot of homework that's worth very little. A very large percentage of the grade was based on exams. Lots of memorizing of formulae.

Paul did a great job in his first semester teaching this challenging course. Despite the complexity of the material, he explained concepts clearly in lectures and was very responsive via email, helping me with a very pressing issue which I am extremely grateful for. Like any math course, be prepared to work hard and stay on top of the coursework.

He was an okay prof - some concepts explained well, others not so much. He sometimes skipped examples, which wasn't helpful. Tons of homework - weekly we assign and long written assignments that easily took hours each week. Quizzes and midterms were fair. Final was tough.
He was approachable outside of class.

Paul is a good teacher who does his best to explain difficult concepts. summer 2024 was his 1st time teaching this course and he did his best. Our averages were fairly low but the class got scaled at the end, which bumped me up a couple letter grades. he's easy to approach and alway willing to answer questions.

Very fair quizzes, as they are directly based on assignment questions. Exams are also quite fair, though they can be a bit long. He's quite helpful during office hours, recommend coming to them if stuck. Lectures are pretty good for the most part, with different examples given.

Paul isn't the worst prof, as I had "worser profs." However, he isn't all that good either. His lectures are boring, he yaps a lot, and his notes are very messy and doesn't cover every math concept we need to do. In addition, his midterms are challenging and assigns a lot of homework. He is a nice guy at least. Personally, I wouldn't recommend him.

He's a pretty good prof overall. Sometimes his explanations of concepts are a bit iffy, but overall a great person. Exams are fair, and quizzes are taken right out of the homework assignments. Would recommend this prof.

This professor gives the worst in class examples as well he rarely actually explains the concepts and says to do it at home instead of him teaching us how to do it. Very good understanding of the concepts in the course, but a very poor professor who I would never recommend to another student hoping to learn something from his lectures.

Paul is one of the few professors who really seems to care about teaching. He posts lecture notes online so you don't have to worry if you've missed the occasional class, but I still recommend going as he's a great explainer and will pause lectures to patiently answer any questions you have as thoroughly as possible before continuing.

Paul is the kind of prof that gets you interested in a course because of him. He's amazing, extremely knowledgable and approachable and truly cares about students. If a lot of people got an assignment question wrong or found it hard he'd spend more time on the concept next lecture for more practice. I felt very supported by him and learned a lot!

(Actually MATH426). Dr. Tupper's kind & humble. It's important to him that students understand concepts, so he welcomes questions & pauses lectures to make clarifications before moving on. He allowed us to allocate more percentage towards our project over our final if we wished. Homework was sometimes tricky, but office hours helped immensely.
paul is a pretty good person, accessible outside the classroom, respect.
